summary: Joel follows up on his previous calendar software evaluation to highlight Airset, an online calendar he overlooked. He finds it significantly better than the hyped alternatives he previously reviewed. He admits he was deterred from trying it because the website lacked screenshots and easy trial access, and because a reviewer had chosen a competitor. He acknowledges this was a mistake and notes he still has feature requests for the product.
key_points: Airset is a superior online calendar compared to the overhyped 'proof-of-conceptware' others were excited about
Poor product marketing (no screenshots, no easy trial) can cause potential users to skip even good products
Letting a single reviewer's opinion substitute for personal evaluation is a mistake
Even knowledgeable users take shortcuts in product evaluation that lead to missed opportunities
money_quotes: It's much, much better than the proof-of-conceptware that people are hyperventilating about.
I went to the website, and couldn't find any screenshots or specific technical information, or an easy way to try it without signing up, so I gave up.
key_insight: A product's marketing and onboarding experience matters as much as its quality — even savvy users will skip great software if they can't quickly see what it does or try it without friction.
